title: fix(core): flush reasoning deltas before clearing on non-reasoning chunks

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(@mastra/core): fix reasoning content loss due to interleaved chunks
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: predecessor — fixed reasoning loss from text-start interleaving; this PR extends the same pattern to tool-input-start and other non-reasoning chunk types

relatedIssues.title: [BUG] reasoning_content missing in multi-turn requests when using thinking models via OpenAI-compatible provider (kimi-k2.5 / DeepSeek-R1)

summary: Fixes a bug where reasoning deltas are lost when non-reasoning chunks (e.g., tool-input-start) arrive before reasoning-end during streaming with OpenAI-compatible thinking models (kimi-k2.5, DeepSeek-R1). The provider emits reasoning-end in flush(), so it arrives after tool-call chunks. The existing guard code cleared reasoningDeltas without saving them first, causing empty reasoning_content and 400 errors on subsequent turns. The fix applies the same flush-before-clear pattern already used for textDeltas: save accumulated reasoning deltas to messageList before resetting state. Includes a well-written regression test covering the exact interleaving scenario.
maintainerNotes: High-quality community PR from an AI-assisted contributor. The fix is minimal, well-scoped, and mirrors an existing pattern (textDeltas flush at lines 123-144 of llm-execution-step.ts). The regression test is thorough and covers the exact failure scenario. Missing a changeset — author will need to add one. CI is still pending (core tests, memory tests, etc. haven't run yet). CodeRabbit review had only one nitpick suggesting an additional replay-path assertion. PR #11494 (merged Dec 2025) fixed the same class of bug for text-start interleaving; this PR extends the fix to tool-input-start and other non-reasoning chunk types. The linked issue #13635 has a detailed root cause analysis that matches the fix exactly.
